title: T-073 — Shared BOM SSOT + released edit clone-on-write enforcement
prompt:
# T-073 — Shared BOM SSOT + released edit clone-on-write enforcement

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5.1A, §7.1, §7.4, §7.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Shared BOM SSOT backend contract with released edit clone-on-write and Technical approval guards.

Use existing BOM tables from T-002 if present; add source/review metadata through a safe migration only if missing. Approval flow is draft -> in_review -> approved -> active -> superseded. Production snapshots continue to use ADR-002 immutable snapshots. The service must expose typed decisions so UI can show exact blocking reason. This task is backend/API only. Use the shared release state contract from T-081 where available; otherwise implement a local adapter that can be replaced by T-081. BOM approval must be bundle-aware with factory_spec approval: callers must be able to request a combined FactorySpec+BOM release decision and receive atomic approve/reject semantics.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_bom_ssot_metadata.sql [create]
- apps/web/actions/technical/boms/*.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/technical/bom-service.ts [create]
- tests/actions/technical/bom-ssot.test.ts [create]
- tests/db/bom-ssot-metadata.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given NPD Builder creates an initial BOM, when it is saved, then it is stored in bom_headers/bom_lines with source npd_builder and status draft.
2. Given an active BOM v4, when a user edits header or lines, then BOM v5 draft is created and v4 remains byte-for-byte unchanged.
3. Given a BOM approval request with failing RM usability, then approval is rejected with the failing checks listed.
4. Given repository search for new BOM tables, then no module-specific NPD/Production BOM table is added by this task.
5. Given a FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval is requested, then BOM and factory_spec release decisions are evaluated together and partial release is rejected unless explicitly split by a Technical approver.
6. Given shared release status model exists, then BOM status mapping uses it without inventing a conflicting enum.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for active BOM immutability and NPD initial draft path.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/actions/technical/bom-ssot.test.ts tests/db/bom-ssot-metadata.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not create a second BOM model.
- Do not update active BOM rows in place.
- Do not bypass ADR-002 snapshots.
- Do not approve a BOM for factory use when the paired factory_spec is not approved_for_factory/released.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
